title: Value Network — NAICS 531120 Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings

sections.title: CFJ (Industry-Level)
sections.content:
> **Provide operationally-ready, leasable non-residential space with reliable building infrastructure and tenant services, under a long-term rental/lease contract, at a risk-adjusted yield that funds capex and asset appreciation.**

Mechanism-free: no reference to specific asset class, technology, or financing vehicle.

---

sections.title: L7 → L6 Process Structure
sections.content:
| Level | Node | Type | Job family | Serves output types |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| L7 | Commercial Real Estate Ecosystem | — | Enable lessor-tenant value exchange | All |
| L6a | Site Acquisition & Development | Core | Acquire and entitle developable parcel | Office, Logistics, Retail, Industrial, MedOffice |
| L6b | Building Construction & Fit-Out | Core | Construct envelope and core-and-shell | All |
| L6c | Leasing & Tenant Onboarding | Core | Market, underwrite, sign, onboard tenants | All |
| L6d | Property Operations & Infrastructure | Core | **Deliver continuous building services** (where BESS sits) | All |
| L6e | Tenant Services & Turnover | Core | Service, renew, rotate tenants | All |
| L6f | Asset Disposal / Recapitalization | Core | Refinance or divest at target yield | All |
| L6H-FM | Facilities Management (H) | Horizontal | Maintain physical asset in rentable condition | All |
| L6H-ESG | ESG & Compliance Reporting (H) | Horizontal | Produce EU Taxonomy / GRESB / CSRD dossiers | All |
| L6H-FIN | Financial Administration (H) | Horizontal | Rent roll, CAM reconciliation, debt service | All |
| L6H-INS | Insurance & Risk (H) | Horizontal | Underwrite fire/liability/BI exposure | All |

---

sections.title: Sub-SoS Decomposition under L6d (Property Operations)
sections.content:
Mein Solar sells into **L6d-a Building Energy Infrastructure**.

| L6a-c | Node | Description |
|---|---|---|
| L6d-a | **Building Energy Infrastructure** | On-site generation, storage, conversion, grid coupling |
| L6d-b | Building Automation & Controls | BMS/BAS, access, lighting, security platforms |
| L6d-c | Utilities Provisioning | Water, wastewater, submetering, telecom |
| L6d-d | Janitorial, Waste & Landscaping | Physical upkeep of common areas and grounds |

---

sections.title: L5 Positions under L6d-a Building Energy Infrastructure (Mein Solar's Segment)
sections.content:
Sibling L5 alternatives under same L6 parent — the competitive context.

| L5 Position | Status | Typical Incumbents (DE) | Mein Solar? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Battery Energy Storage System** | — | Voltfang, Tesvolt, Commeo, Sungrow, Tesla Megapack, Fluence | **← PRIMARY POSITION** |
| **Rooftop / Carport PV Generator** | — | Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck Solar, IBC Solar, Mein Solar | **← SECONDARY POSITION** |
| **EV Charging Infrastructure (AC + HPC)** | — | Alpitronic, ads-tec, Kostad, ABB E-mobility, EnBW/Ionity OEM | **← SECONDARY (via buffer feature)** |
| HVAC / Heat Pump Plant | — | Viessmann, Vaillant, Daikin, Mitsubishi Electric, STIEBEL ELTRON | — |
| CHP / Cogeneration Unit | — | 2G Energy, Sokratherm, MTU Onsite, Viessmann Vitobloc | — |
| MV Grid Connection & Switchgear | — | Siemens, ABB, Schneider, Eaton | — |
| Building Energy Management System (BEMS) | — | Siemens Desigo, Schneider EcoStruxure, Honeywell Niagara, Kieback&Peter | — |
| Emergency / Standby Generator | — | Caterpillar, Kohler, FG Wilson, Rolls-Royce MTU | — |

---

sections.title: L4 Subsystems under Mein Solar's L5 (BESS)
sections.content:
| L4 | Function | Typical vendor |
|---|---|---|
| Battery Modules & BMS | Store/release DC energy, state monitoring | CATL, EVE, BYD, Samsung SDI |
| Power Conversion System (PCS) | 4-quadrant DC↔AC bidirectional inverter | Sungrow, SMA, Power Electronics, Ingeteam |
| MV Transformer & Switchgear | Step-up to 10/20 kV DSO grid | Siemens, ABB, Schneider |
| Thermal Management Unit | Liquid cooling / HVAC inside container | Pfannenberg, Rittal, Kelvion |
| Fire Suppression System | NOVEC 1230 / aerosol, deflagration vents | Siemens, Fike, Minimax |
| Energy Management Software (EMS) | Dispatch vs. peak-shaving / arbitrage setpoints | Envision, Next Kraftwerke, Entrix, Kiwigrid |
| Container Enclosure & Racking | IP54 housing, seismic frame, cable management | Sungrow, SolarEast, BYD, CATL EnerC |

---

sections.title: L6 → Output Type Activation Matrix
sections.content:
Output types = lessor asset classes (what the landlord produces).

|  | Office | Logistics / Industrial | Retail | Med-Office | Data-Center Shell |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L6a Site Acquisition | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| L6b Construction | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| L6c Leasing | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| L6d-a Energy Infrastructure | ✓ | ✓✓ | ✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ |
| L6d-b BMS/BAS | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓✓ |
| L6e Tenant Services | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — |
| L6f Disposal | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

Energy infra activation is **highest in logistics, med-office, and data-center-shell** — these are where Mein Solar's BESS fit is strongest (high kW loads, EV fleet charging, resilience demand).

---

sections.title: 04e — Direct Competence Transfer Scan (Mein Solar)
sections.content:
Scan across the entire 531120 VN for where Mein Solar's existing EPC capability transfers with LOW/MEDIUM adaptation.

| VN Unit | Level | Competence Required | Has It? | Effort | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rooftop / Carport PV Generator | L5 | PV EPC, MV coupling, Baugenehmigung | YES | LOW | Mein Solar's incumbent capability; same customer |
| EV Charging Infrastructure | L5 | MV coupling, civil, HPC integration | PARTIAL | LOW | Feature 7 already defined; dispenser sourcing via OEM |
| BEMS (BESS+PV+HVAC orchestration) | L5 | EMS software integration at site scope | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Has EMS for BESS; needs BMS/BAS bridge |
| MV Grid Connection & Switchgear | L5 | MV design, protection relays, DSO interface | YES | LOW | Already delivered inside BESS scope |
| CHP / Cogeneration | L5 | Prime-mover engineering, gas permits | NO | HIGH | excluded |
| HVAC / Heat-Pump Plant | L5 | Refrigerant handling, hydronic design | NO | HIGH | excluded |
| Emergency Generator | L5 | Diesel/gas genset integration | PARTIAL | MEDIUM | Parallel coupling via PCS feasible |

---

sections.title: 04f — Strategic Adjacency Expansion (within L6d-a)
sections.content:
Horizontal moves inside Mein Solar's home segment.

| Adjacent L5 | Current Incumbents | Competence Gap | Investment | Fit (1-10) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rooftop / Carport PV | Enerparc, BayWa r.e., IBC Solar | None — Mein Solar incumbent | 0 | **10** | Already positioned; 100% overlap |
| EV Charging Infrastructure | Alpitronic, ads-tec, Kostad | HPC dispenser certification partnership | 6–12mo, ~€0.5M | **8** | 70% overlap (MV, civil, EMS); dispenser = OEM buy |
| BEMS integrator layer | Siemens Desigo, EcoStruxure, Niagara | BMS/BAS software stack, ISO 16484 | 12–18mo, ~€1.5M | 6 | 40% overlap; entry via PV+BESS+HVAC orchestration niche |
| Emergency Generator integration | CAT, Kohler, Rolls-Royce MTU | Diesel genset OEM partnership | 6–12mo, ~€0.3M | 6 | BESS + genset hybrid for resilience sites |
| HVAC / Heat Pump Plant | Viessmann, Vaillant, Daikin | TGA planning, refrigerant license | >24mo, >€3M | 2 | excluded — <30% overlap |
| CHP / Cogeneration | 2G Energy, Sokratherm | Prime mover, gas regulation | >24mo, >€3M | 2 | excluded — <30% overlap |

---

sections.title: 04g — Vertical Move-Up Assessment
sections.content:
| Current | Target (1 up) | What to Add | Competitors | Feasibility | Economic Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| L5 BESS (under L6d-a) | **L6d-a Integrator**: full Building Energy Infrastructure bundle (PV + BESS + EV + BEMS + MV) | BEMS software layer, genset partnership, multi-asset SLA wrapper, energy-as-a-service financing | Naturstrom+, Enpal Business, Voltaro, Spie, Engie Deutschland | **MEDIUM** | TAM at L6d-a ≈ €5–8B DE commercial annually vs. ~€1–1.5B BESS-only. PV+BESS is natural bundle; BEMS is feasible via OEM tie-up; EaaS financing requires balance-sheet partner. Move-up is coherent with Mein Solar's existing PV+BESS authority. |

Going up one more level (to **L6d Property Operations**) would require full FM service, waste management, security — organizationally distinct; LOW feasibility, not assessed here.

---

sections.title: Completeness Check
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| CFJ industry-level, mechanism-free | ✅ |
| L6 core sequence follows process order | ✅ (Acquire → Build → Lease → Operate → Service → Dispose) |
| Horizontal L6 nodes identified (FM, ESG, FIN, INS) | ✅ |
| L5 alternatives at company's level enumerated | ✅ (8 L5 positions under L6d-a) |
| L4 children ≥3 for Mein Solar's L5 | ✅ (7 L4 subsystems) |
| No solution-specific L7/L6 names | ✅ |
| Company primary position marked | ✅ |
| Unit count in range (15–60) | ✅ (34 units) |

---

sections.title: Key Market Signals (2026)
sections.content:
| Signal | Source |
|---|---|
| C&I storage = **>60% of DE installed storage capacity** (>15 GW in 2024) | PVB/Montel market reports |
| **MiSpeL regulation** mid-2026 enables mixed-energy co-location w/o EEG-subsidy loss | Energy-Storage News |
| EnWG amendment Nov 2025 recognizes large BESS as **privileged infrastructure under building law** — faster Baugenehmigung | Energy-Storage News |
| First-year depreciation **30% → 50%** for C&I BESS from 2025; +10% corp-tax discount for VDE 2510-certified assets | PVB market guide |
| DE grid-connected storage: ~24 GWh (+22% YoY) | Strategic Energy Europe |

---
